description: A guide may walk beside someone through an unfamiliar garden or help them follow a new course of action. 'kulo' offers direction from knowledge of the way and accompanies another person's movement; authority or control needs its own statement. 'thumela' (teach) centres learning, while 'rilowa' (assign) designates an expected performer.
articulatory_notes: Form unvoiced 'k' with a velar closure, then release it into stressed high-back rounded 'u' and keep the lips rounded. The second syllable uses an alveolar lateral 'l'. Voice passes along the tongue's sides and settles into rounded mid-back 'o'.
sound_symbolism: The firm velar opening releases almost at once into a liquid syllable. It sets a direction, then leaves space beside it.
usage_notes: The person being guided comes before the verb. A path or setting can be introduced with the appropriate spatial preposition, as in 'thue' (through). A guide as a person is 'rena kulo miona', unless a more specific role such as 'lumishe' (mentor) fits.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Guidance leaves practical movement with the traveller. It offers knowledge of a way without dressing command or dominion in a gentler title.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Knowledge of a route often lives in repeated travel through a particular place. A guide shares that situated knowledge by going with the person who needs it.

semantic_domains.community: an accompanying relation between a knowledgeable guide and another traveller
semantic_domains.spatial: direction offered along a physical or conceptual path
semantic_domains.wisdom: practical knowledge of a way shared through accompaniment
